response.justification: The candidate brings a genuinely relevant geospatial foundation — founding and maintaining the GISCollective geo-data platform since 2009, building GIS web applications for Green Map, and speaking at FOSS4G — plus proven low-latency systems work at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, microservices handling millions of requests per second). However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his professional C++ experience dates to 2008 at National Instruments; his systems-language depth is in DLang, and his recent decade has centered on TypeScript/Node.js product and AI-tooling work. He also has no direct routing engine, tile generation, or navigation guidance experience, making him an adjacent rather than direct fit.
strengths: Long-running geospatial domain experience (GISCollective founder, Green Map GIS apps, FOSS4G speaker)
Proven high-performance/low-latency systems work in DLang with async I/O, caching, and microservices at scale
Broad full-stack versatility, Linux/Docker/Kubernetes operations, and strong testing/craftsmanship discipline
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience; last professional C++ work was in 2008
No direct routing, tiling, or navigation guidance pipeline experience
Recent focus (2018-present) is TypeScript/Node.js product and AI tooling, away from systems-level data pipelines
